products.title: 6" Strelitzia Plant – Tropical Foliage Houseplant
products.body_html:
The 6-inch Strelitzia is a young tropical plant admired for its strong upright growth and broad, architectural leaves. Often grown as a statement foliage plant, Strelitzia brings a clean, modern, tropical feel to bright indoor spaces. While compact at this size, it matures into a bold centerpiece over time with proper care, making it a great long-term houseplant investment.

Care Guide

🌞 Light

  • Bright, indirect light is ideal

  • Can handle some gentle direct morning or late-afternoon sun

  • Low light will slow growth and reduce leaf size

💧 Water

  • Water when the top 2 inches of soil feel dry

  • Keep soil evenly moist, not soggy

  • Always allow excess water to drain fully

🌡 Temperature & Humidity

  • Prefers warm environments

  • Ideal temperature range: 65–85°F

  • Enjoys moderate humidity but adapts well to normal indoor air

🌿 Soil

  • Well-draining potting mix

  • A standard indoor plant mix with added perlite works well

🍃 General Care

  • Leaf splitting is normal as the plant matures

  • Rotate plant occasionally for even growth

  • Wipe leaves gently to remove dust and keep foliage healthy

products.title: 4" African Violet (Saintpaulia)
products.body_html:
The 4-inch African Violet is a classic indoor favorite, admired for its soft, velvety leaves and delicate blooms in shades of purple, pink, or white. Compact and elegant, it adds a touch of charm to windowsills, desks, or plant displays while thriving in gentle indoor light.

🌿 Care Guide:

  • Light: Bright, indirect sunlight; avoid direct rays that may scorch leaves.

  • Water: Keep soil evenly moist—use room-temperature water and avoid wetting the leaves. Water from the base if possible.

  • Humidity: Prefers moderate to high humidity. Place on a tray of pebbles with water for extra moisture.

  • Temperature: Maintain 65–75°F (18–24°C).

  • Soil: Use a well-draining African violet or light potting mix.

  • Fertilizer: Feed every 2–4 weeks with a balanced, water-soluble fertilizer during active growth.

products.title: Tulip Bulbs – Spring Flowering Perennial Bulbs
products.body_html:
Tulip bulbs are a classic choice for adding bold, vibrant color to spring gardens, landscapes, and containers. Known for their clean, elegant blooms and wide range of colors, tulips are perfect for borders, mass plantings, and seasonal displays. These bulbs are easy to plant and create a striking spring show when planted in groups.

Planting & Care Guide

🌞 Light

  • Full sun to partial shade

  • Best flowering with at least 6 hours of sunlight

🌱 Planting

  • Plant in fall before the first hard frost

  • Plant pointed end up

  • Depth: 6–8 inches

  • Spacing: 4–6 inches apart

💧 Water

  • Water well after planting

  • Keep soil lightly moist during growth

  • Avoid overwatering, especially in poorly draining soil

🌡 Temperature & Climate

  • Require a cold period to bloom

  • Best suited for temperate climates

  • In warm regions, pre-chilling bulbs may be needed

🌿 Soil

  • Well-draining soil is essential

  • Sandy or loamy soil works best

  • Avoid soggy or compacted soil

🌸 After Bloom Care

  • Remove spent flowers after blooming

  • Allow foliage to yellow naturally before cutting back

  • Bulbs may be treated as annuals in warmer climates

products.title: Daffodil Bulbs – Spring Flowering Perennial Bulbs 10PCS
products.body_html:

Buying in bulk is ideal for mass plantings, borders, naturalized areas, or seasonal retail displays. These bulbs are hardy, low-maintenance, and well suited for beginner and experienced gardeners alike.

Planting & Care Guide

🌞 Light

  • Full sun to partial shade

  • Best flowering occurs with at least 6 hours of sunlight daily

🌱 Planting

  • Plant bulbs in fall before the ground freezes

  • Plant pointed end up

  • Depth: 4–6 inches deep

  • Spacing: 4–6 inches apart

💧 Water

  • Water thoroughly after planting

  • Keep soil lightly moist during active growth

  • Reduce watering after flowering as foliage begins to die back

🌡 Temperature & Hardiness

  • Cold-hardy and frost tolerant

  • Suitable for most temperate climates

  • Requires a cold period to bloom properly

🌿 Soil

  • Well-draining soil is essential

  • Avoid heavy, waterlogged soil

  • Garden soil amended with compost works well

🌸 After Bloom Care

  • Leave foliage intact until it yellows naturally

  • Do not cut leaves early, as they store energy for next season

  • Bulbs will multiply over time with proper care
